seoTitle: How to Create a Letterhead Design in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to create a professional letterhead design in Canva, incorporating your logo, colors, and typography for business documents.
intro: A well-designed letterhead reinforces brand identity on every document you send, from proposals to official correspondence. Canva makes it easy to design one using templates and your existing brand assets. This guide covers building a letterhead from start to finish.

steps.heading: Choose a letterhead template
steps.body: Search Canva's templates for 'letterhead' and select a layout that fits your business style.

steps.heading: Add your logo
steps.body: Upload your company logo and place it in the header area of the template.

steps.heading: Set your brand colors and fonts
steps.body: Update the color palette and typography to match your existing brand guidelines.

steps.heading: Include contact and business details
steps.body: Add your address, phone number, email, or website in the footer or header as needed.

steps.heading: Save as a reusable template
steps.body: Keep a master version so you can quickly create new letters or documents using the same design.

faq.question: Can I use my letterhead design as a Word document template?
faq.answer: Canva itself is design-focused, but you can export a blank version as an image or PDF background to use in other document software.

faq.question: Are letterhead templates free in Canva?
faq.answer: Many are free, with additional premium options available under Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same letterhead for different documents?
faq.answer: Yes, duplicate the base design and update only the content each time you need a new document.

faq.question: What file format should I export my letterhead in?
faq.answer: PDF is common for printed correspondence, while PNG works well if you're embedding it as a background image.
